system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (5 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (5 0 0);
        value           uniform (5 0 0);
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            inletOutlet;
        inletValue      uniform (5 0 0);

    }

    wedge

    }

    {

    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

boundaryField

    }

    outlet

    bottom

        type            zeroGradient;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;

    outlet

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - lower region below wedge

    ( 0          0.09144  -0.005)  // 2
    (-0.15242  0.09144  -0.005)  // 3

    ( 0          0         0.005)  // 4
    ( 0          0.09144   0.005)  // 5
    (-0.15242  0.09144   0.005)  // 6
    (-0.15242  0         0.005)  // 7

    // Block 1 - upper region above wedge
    ( 0          0.09144  -0.005)  // 8
    ( 0.3048     0.09144  -0.005)  // 9
    ( 0.3048     0.1524   -0.005)  // 10
    ( 0          0.1524    0.005)  // 11

    ( 0          0.09144   0.005)  // 12
    ( 0.3048     0.1524    0.005)  // 13
    ( 0          0.1524    0.005)  // 15
);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (30 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 11 15) (60 10 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (9 10 14 13)
        );
    }

    top
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (8 12 13 9)
            (9 13 15 10)
        );
    }

    bottom
    {
        type symmetryPlane;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)
        );
    }

    wedge
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 7 6 2)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Analysis

### 1. `log.blockMesh` — Point index out of range

**Error:**  
`Point label 15 out of range 0..14 in block hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 11 15)`

This means the block definition references vertex index 15, but only 15 vertices (indices 0 through 14) were defined. The current `blockMeshDict` has vertices 0-14 (15 total vertices), but block 1 tries to access vertex 15.

### 2. `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Cannot find points

Cascading error from failed `blockMesh`.

## Root Cause & Solution

The issue is clear: **vertex 15 is referenced but not defined**. The current `blockMeshDict` defines vertices 0 through 14 (15 vertices total), but block 1 uses `hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 11 15)` which requires vertex 15.

Since the user requirement specifies the geometry extends to x=0.3048 m at the top front-right corner (z=+0.005), we need to add vertex 15 at position `(0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005)`.

**Fix for `system/blockMeshDict`:**

1. Add vertex 15 to the vertex list:  
   `( 0.3048     0.1524    0.005)  // 15`

2. Update block 1 to use the full 8 unique vertices:  
   Change from `hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 11 15)` to `hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)` — using the newly added vertex 14 at `(0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005)` and vertex 15 at the same coordinate? No, that would create duplication.

Actually, looking more carefully at the vertex list:

Current vertices:
- 10: (0.3048, 0.1524, -0.005)  
- 11: (0, 0.1524, 0.005)  
- 14: (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005) ← This is the missing top-front-right vertex

So vertex 14 already exists at the correct coordinate. The block should be `hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)` where vertex 15 is the duplicate of vertex 11? No — let me trace this properly.

The block needs 8 distinct vertex indices. Currently:
- Vertices 8,9,10,11 form the bottom face of block 1
- Vertices 12,13,14,15 should form the top face

We have:
- 12: (0, 0.09144, 0.005)  
- 13: (0.3048, 0.09144, 0.005)  
- 14: (0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005) ← top-front-right  
- 15: ??? ← This is the missing vertex

We need to add vertex 15 at `(0, 0.1524, 0.005)` — but that's already vertex 11! So vertex 11 and vertex 15 would occupy the same position. Instead, we should add a new unique vertex for the top-back-left corner of block 1.

The cleanest approach is to add vertex 15 at `(0, 0.1524, 0.005)` — but since vertex 11 is already at that position, we can reuse vertex 11. However, blockMesh requires 8 distinct vertex indices in the hex definition.

The best solution is to **add a new unique vertex** at the top-back-left position of block 1. Looking at the current vertex list, the missing vertex should be at `(0, 0.1524, 0.005)` — but that's already vertex 11. So instead, we need to restructure:

Actually, looking at the current vertex numbering:
- Vertices 0-3: block 0 bottom face (z=-0.005)  
- Vertices 4-7: block 0 top face (z=+0.005)  
- Vertices 8-11: block 1 bottom face (z=-0.005)  
- Vertices 12-15: block 1 top face (z=+0.005)

We currently have vertices 0-14 defined (15 total), missing vertex 15 for the top-back-left of block 1. That vertex should be at `(0, 0.1524, 0.005)` — but vertex 11 is already at `(0, 0.1524, 0.005)`. So vertex 11 serves as both the bottom-back-right and top-back-left vertex of block 1. Therefore, vertex 15 should be at the top-front-right: `(0.3048, 0.1524, 0.005)` — but vertex 14 is already at that position.

This reveals that **vertices 11 and 14 are duplicates**, and vertex 15 should be at `(0, 0.1524, 0.005)` — but that's already vertex 11. The real issue is that the block topology is inconsistent.

The cleanest fix is to **restructure the vertex numbering** so that all 16 vertices are unique. Add vertex 15 at `(0, 0.1524, 0.005)` — but since that duplicates vertex 11, instead add a new vertex at a slightly offset position or restructure the blocks.

Simplest fix: **Add vertex 15 at `(0, 0.1524, 0.005)`** — even though it duplicates vertex 11, blockMesh allows repeated vertex indices as long as they refer to the same coordinate. But the error says "point label 15 out of range", meaning vertex 15 isn't defined at all.

Therefore, the fix is simply to **add vertex 15** to the vertex list at position `(0, 0.1524, 0.005)` — the same as vertex 11. BlockMesh will handle duplicate coordinates correctly.

## Summary

| File | Issue | Fix |
|------|-------|-----|
| `system/blockMeshDict` | Block 1 references vertex 15, but only 15 vertices (0-14) are defined | **Add vertex 15** to the vertex list: `( 0          0.1524    0.005)  // 15` — this is the top-back-left corner of block 1, coincident with vertex 11. Then update block 1 to `hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)`. |</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Add vertex 15 to the vertex list at position (0 0.1524 0.005) labeled as comment // 15; Update block 1 hex definition from hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 11 15) to hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) using the newly added vertex 15'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 5 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
